name: OpenAI
title: Build a website with your OpenAI API key
description: Connect your OpenAI API key to OpenThorn and build complete websites with GPT-5.5 or GPT-5.4 — paying OpenAI's raw per-token rates, with no subscription or markup.
answer: Yes — you can build a complete website with your own OpenAI API key. Create a key at platform.openai.com, paste it into OpenThorn (free, encrypted server-side), describe your site, and the agent generates, previews, and deploys it. You pay OpenAI's published per-token rates directly — typically cents to a few dollars per site.
freeTier: No free API tier — OpenAI requires prepaid credit (minimum $5), which doubles as a hard spend cap.

models.name: GPT-5.5
models.note: Flagship model — strongest reasoning and code quality for complex, multi-page sites.

models.name: GPT-5.4
models.note: Excellent quality at a lower price; a solid default for most websites.

models.name: GPT-5.4 mini
models.note: Fast and cheap — good for simple sites and quick iterations.

costNote: With GPT-5.4 mini, a typical landing page costs a few cents in tokens; with GPT-5.5, expect roughly $0.50–$3 per complete site depending on size and revisions. Check OpenAI's pricing page for current per-token rates.

steps.name: Create an OpenAI API key
steps.text: Sign in at platform.openai.com, add at least $5 of prepaid credit, and create a new secret key under API keys. Copy it immediately — it is shown only once.

steps.name: Set a spend limit
steps.text: In the OpenAI billing settings, set a monthly budget so usage can never exceed what you choose to prepay.

steps.name: Connect the key to OpenThorn
steps.text: Sign up free at openthorn.app, open the Providers page, choose OpenAI, and paste your key. It is encrypted server-side with AES-256-GCM and never exposed raw.

steps.name: Describe your website and ship it
steps.text: Create a project, pick a GPT model, and describe the site in plain language. Preview it live in the browser, iterate conversationally, then deploy to Netlify or export the source.

faqs.question: How much does it cost to build a website with the OpenAI API?
faqs.answer: You pay OpenAI's per-token rates only — OpenThorn adds no markup and no subscription. A typical site costs cents with GPT-5.4 mini and roughly $0.50–$3 with GPT-5.5, versus $20–50/month for credit-based AI builders.

faqs.question: Is my OpenAI API key safe in OpenThorn?
faqs.answer: Keys are encrypted server-side with AES-256-GCM using per-user derived keys and are never exposed raw to the browser. You can also set a spend limit in your OpenAI dashboard and rotate or revoke the key at any time.

faqs.question: Which OpenAI model should I pick for website generation?
faqs.answer: GPT-5.4 is the best default: near-flagship code quality at a lower price. Use GPT-5.5 for complex multi-page sites with custom logic, and GPT-5.4 mini when speed and minimal cost matter more than polish.

name: Anthropic
title: Build a website with your Anthropic (Claude) API key
description: Connect your Anthropic API key to OpenThorn and build complete websites with Claude — paying Anthropic's raw per-token rates, with no subscription or markup.
answer: Yes — you can build a complete website with your own Anthropic API key. Create a key at console.anthropic.com, paste it into OpenThorn (free, encrypted server-side), and describe your site. Claude models are consistently among the strongest for code generation, and you pay Anthropic's published rates directly — typically under $2 per site with Claude Sonnet.
freeTier: No standing free API tier — Anthropic uses prepaid credit (minimum $5), which acts as a natural spend cap.

models.name: Claude Sonnet 4.6
models.note: The sweet spot for web generation — excellent code quality at $3/M input, $15/M output tokens.

models.name: Claude Fable 5
models.note: Anthropic's most capable model, for the most demanding multi-page builds.

models.name: Claude Haiku 4.5
models.note: Fast and inexpensive — great for simple sites and rapid iteration.

costNote: With Claude Sonnet 4.6 ($3/M input, $15/M output tokens), a complete landing page typically costs $0.50–$2 in API usage. Claude Haiku 4.5 brings that down to cents.

steps.name: Create an Anthropic API key
steps.text: Sign in at console.anthropic.com, add prepaid credit (from $5), and create a key under Settings → API keys. Copy it immediately — it is shown only once.

steps.name: Set a spend limit
steps.text: Prepaid credit is itself a hard cap; you can also configure monthly spend limits in the console so costs stay predictable.

steps.name: Connect the key to OpenThorn
steps.text: Sign up free at openthorn.app, open the Providers page, choose Anthropic, and paste your key. It is encrypted server-side with AES-256-GCM and never exposed raw.

steps.name: Describe your website and ship it
steps.text: Create a project, pick a Claude model, and describe the site. Preview it live, iterate conversationally, then deploy to Netlify or export the full source.

faqs.question: How much does it cost to build a website with the Claude API?
faqs.answer: You pay Anthropic's per-token rates only. With Claude Sonnet 4.6 a typical site costs $0.50–$2; with Claude Haiku 4.5, cents. There is no OpenThorn subscription or markup — compare that with $25+/month for credit-based builders.

faqs.question: Which Claude model is best for building websites?
faqs.answer: Claude Sonnet 4.6 is the best default — it is one of the strongest coding models available at mid-tier pricing. Choose Claude Fable 5 for the most complex builds, or Claude Haiku 4.5 when cost and speed matter most.

faqs.question: Is my Anthropic API key safe in OpenThorn?
faqs.answer: Keys are encrypted server-side with AES-256-GCM using per-user derived keys and are never exposed raw to the browser. Prepaid credit caps your maximum exposure, and you can rotate or revoke the key in the Anthropic console at any time.

name: Google Gemini
title: Build a website with your Google Gemini API key
description: Connect your Google Gemini API key to OpenThorn and build complete websites — Gemini has a genuinely free API tier, so you can build your first site for $0.
answer: Yes — and Gemini is the cheapest way to start: Google AI Studio issues free API keys with a no-cost tier, so you can build your first website for $0. Create a key at aistudio.google.com, paste it into OpenThorn (free, encrypted server-side), describe your site, and deploy it.
freeTier: Yes — Google AI Studio offers a free tier with daily request limits, no credit card required. Paid tiers unlock higher limits and the strongest models.

models.name: Gemini 3.5 Flash
models.note: Fast, capable, and very cheap — the best default for website generation.

models.name: Gemini 2.5 Pro
models.note: Stronger reasoning for complex sites; higher per-token price.

models.name: Gemini 2.5 Flash-Lite
models.note: The cheapest option — fine for simple pages and experiments.

costNote: On the free tier, your first sites cost $0 within daily limits. On paid tiers, Gemini Flash models are among the cheapest capable models available — a complete site typically costs single-digit cents.

steps.name: Create a Gemini API key
steps.text: Sign in at aistudio.google.com with a Google account and click Get API key. The free tier requires no billing details.

steps.name: Optionally add billing
steps.text: For higher rate limits and access to the strongest models, enable billing in Google AI Studio and set a budget alert in Google Cloud.

steps.name: Connect the key to OpenThorn
steps.text: Sign up free at openthorn.app, open the Providers page, choose Google Gemini, and paste your key. It is encrypted server-side with AES-256-GCM and never exposed raw.

steps.name: Describe your website and ship it
steps.text: Create a project, pick a Gemini model, and describe the site. Preview it live, iterate conversationally, then deploy to Netlify or export the full source.

faqs.question: Can I build a website with AI completely free using Gemini?
faqs.answer: Yes. Google AI Studio's free tier issues API keys with daily request limits and no credit card. Combined with OpenThorn's free platform, you can build and deploy your first website for $0 — the only constraint is the free tier's daily quota.

faqs.question: Which Gemini model should I use for website generation?
faqs.answer: Gemini 3.5 Flash is the best default — fast, capable, and very cheap. Use Gemini 2.5 Pro when a site needs more complex logic, or Flash-Lite for quick experiments at minimal cost.

faqs.question: Is my Gemini API key safe in OpenThorn?
faqs.answer: Keys are encrypted server-side with AES-256-GCM using per-user derived keys and are never exposed raw to the browser. You can revoke or regenerate the key in Google AI Studio at any time.

steps.name: Connect the key to OpenThorn

faqs.question: Is DeepSeek good enough to build a real website?
faqs.answer: Yes. DeepSeek's recent models score competitively with Western flagships on coding benchmarks while costing a fraction per token. For most landing pages and multi-page sites the output quality is strong, and OpenThorn's verification gate catches compile errors before you ship.

faqs.question: How much does a website cost with the DeepSeek API?
faqs.answer: Usually low single-digit cents per complete site — DeepSeek's per-token rates are typically 10–20× lower than flagship Western models. There is no OpenThorn fee on top.

faqs.question: Is my DeepSeek API key safe in OpenThorn?
faqs.answer: Keys are encrypted server-side with AES-256-GCM using per-user derived keys and are never exposed raw to the browser. Your prepaid balance also caps maximum spend by design.

faqs.question: Is my Mistral API key safe in OpenThorn?

name: Groq

description: Connect your Groq API key to OpenThorn and build complete websites at extreme speed — Groq's LPU hardware serves open models hundreds of tokens per second, with a free tier.
answer: Yes — and Groq is the fastest option: its LPU hardware serves open models at hundreds of tokens per second, so the OpenThorn agent iterates dramatically faster than on standard APIs. Groq offers a free API tier; create a key at console.groq.com, paste it into OpenThorn, and build your first site for $0.
freeTier: Yes — Groq has a free tier with daily rate limits, no credit card required.

models.note: Groq's agentic system — a strong default for website builds.

models.name: GPT OSS 120B
models.note: OpenAI's open-weight model served at Groq speed — great quality per dollar.

models.name: Llama 3.3 70B Versatile
models.note: Reliable open model, very cheap and fast.

costNote: The free tier covers first sites at $0 within daily limits. Paid rates for open models are very low — a complete website typically costs under ten cents.

steps.name: Create a Groq API key
steps.text: Sign up at console.groq.com and create a key under API Keys. The free tier requires no payment details.

steps.name: Optionally upgrade for higher limits
steps.text: The developer tier unlocks higher rate limits with pay-as-you-go billing; set a spend cap in the console.

steps.name: Connect the key to OpenThorn
steps.text: Sign up free at openthorn.app, open the Providers page, choose Groq, and paste your key. It is encrypted server-side with AES-256-GCM and never exposed raw.

steps.name: Describe your website and ship it
steps.text: Create a project, pick a model, and describe the site. Generation streams at hundreds of tokens per second — preview, iterate, then deploy or export.

faqs.question: Why use Groq for AI website building?
faqs.answer: Speed. Groq's custom LPU hardware serves models at hundreds of tokens per second — often 5–10× faster than standard GPU APIs — so each agent iteration completes in seconds. Combined with the free tier, it is one of the best ways to try OpenThorn.

faqs.question: Is Groq the same as Grok?
faqs.answer: No. Groq is an AI inference company with custom LPU hardware that serves open models extremely fast. Grok is xAI's model family — OpenThorn supports both, as separate providers.

faqs.question: How much does a website cost with the Groq API?
faqs.answer: On the free tier, $0 within daily limits. On paid tiers, open models served by Groq cost so little per token that a complete website is typically under ten cents.

faqs.answer: Sonar models are search-grounded — they can pull current facts from the web while generating, which helps for content-heavy sites that reference real, up-to-date information. For pure code generation, a dedicated coding model may be a better fit.

faqs.question: Do Perplexity Pro subscribers get API credit?

faqs.question: Is my Perplexity API key safe in OpenThorn?

name: Ollama
title: Build a website with Ollama (free local AI)
description: Run open models locally with Ollama and build complete websites in OpenThorn with zero API costs — your prompts and code never leave your machine.
answer: Yes — and it costs nothing: Ollama runs open models like Llama and Qwen 2.5 Coder locally on your own hardware, so there is no API key, no per-token bill, and no data leaving your machine. Install Ollama, pull a model, point OpenThorn at your local endpoint, and build.
freeTier: Entirely free — models run on your own hardware. The only requirement is enough RAM/VRAM for the model you choose.

models.name: Qwen 2.5 Coder
models.note: The best local coding model for its size — recommended default.

models.name: Llama 3.2
models.note: Light and fast on modest hardware.

models.name: Mistral
models.note: Solid all-rounder that runs on most machines.

costNote: $0 — inference runs on your own hardware. Larger models produce better websites but need more RAM/VRAM (a 7B coder model runs on most modern laptops).

steps.name: Install Ollama
steps.text: Download Ollama from ollama.com for Windows, macOS, or Linux and install it — it runs a local server on port 11434.

steps.name: Pull a coding model
steps.text: Run `ollama pull qwen2.5-coder` in a terminal. A 7B model needs roughly 8 GB of RAM; larger variants produce better code.

steps.name: Connect Ollama to OpenThorn
steps.text: Sign up free at openthorn.app, open the Providers page, choose Ollama, and point it at your local endpoint. No API key required.

steps.name: Describe your website and ship it
steps.text: Create a project, pick your local model, and describe the site. Everything runs on your machine; deploy to Netlify or export when ready.

faqs.question: Can I really build a website with AI completely free and offline?
faqs.answer: Yes. Ollama runs open models locally — no API key, no token bills, no cloud. OpenThorn's platform is free, so the entire build costs $0. Quality depends on the model size your hardware can run; local models are weaker than cloud flagships but capable for simpler sites.

faqs.question: What hardware do I need for Ollama?
faqs.answer: A 7B coding model like Qwen 2.5 Coder runs on most modern laptops with 8 GB+ RAM. More RAM or a GPU lets you run larger variants, which noticeably improves generated code quality.

faqs.question: Is local AI private?
faqs.answer: Yes — with Ollama your prompts and generated code never leave your machine. This is the strongest privacy option among all providers OpenThorn supports.

name: Fireworks AI
title: Build a website with your Fireworks AI API key
description: Connect your Fireworks AI API key to OpenThorn and build complete websites with fast-served open models — DeepSeek, Llama, and Qwen at low per-token rates.
answer: Yes — Fireworks AI serves the strongest open-source models (DeepSeek V3.2, Qwen3, Llama 3.3) on fast, optimized infrastructure. Create a key at app.fireworks.ai (new accounts include free credit), paste it into OpenThorn (free, encrypted server-side), and a typical website costs cents in API usage.
freeTier: New accounts include a small free credit; after that, pay-as-you-go at low open-model rates.

models.name: DeepSeek V3.2
models.note: Frontier-class open model — the strongest choice for website builds.

models.name: Qwen3 235B A22B
models.note: Large MoE model with excellent code ability.

models.name: Llama 3.3 70B Instruct
models.note: Fast and cheap for simpler sites.

costNote: Open-model rates on Fireworks are a fraction of proprietary flagship prices — a complete website typically costs cents.

steps.name: Create a Fireworks API key
steps.text: Sign up at app.fireworks.ai — new accounts include free credit — and create a key under API Keys.

steps.name: Set a spend alert
steps.text: Configure billing alerts in the Fireworks dashboard to keep usage visible.

steps.name: Connect the key to OpenThorn
steps.text: Sign up free at openthorn.app, open the Providers page, choose Fireworks AI, and paste your key. It is encrypted server-side with AES-256-GCM and never exposed raw.

steps.name: Describe your website and ship it
steps.text: Create a project, pick an open model, and describe the site. Preview it live, iterate, then deploy to Netlify or export the source.

faqs.question: How much does a website cost with Fireworks AI?
faqs.answer: Typically cents per complete site — Fireworks serves open-source models at rates far below proprietary flagships, new accounts include free credit, and OpenThorn adds no markup.

faqs.question: Which Fireworks model is best for website generation?
faqs.answer: DeepSeek V3.2 is the strongest all-rounder. Qwen3 235B excels at complex code; Llama 3.3 70B is the fast, cheap option for simple sites.

faqs.question: Is my Fireworks API key safe in OpenThorn?
faqs.answer: Keys are encrypted server-side with AES-256-GCM using per-user derived keys and are never exposed raw to the browser. You can rotate keys in the Fireworks dashboard at any time.

name: Cerebras
title: Build a website with your Cerebras API key
description: Connect your Cerebras API key to OpenThorn and build websites at the fastest inference speeds available — wafer-scale hardware serving open models at thousands of tokens per second, with a free tier.
answer: Yes — Cerebras serves open models at thousands of tokens per second on wafer-scale hardware, the fastest inference commercially available. It offers a free API tier, so you can build your first OpenThorn website for $0 with near-instant agent iterations. Create a key at cloud.cerebras.ai and paste it into OpenThorn.
freeTier: Yes — a free tier with daily token limits, no credit card required.

models.name: GPT OSS 120B
models.note: OpenAI's open-weight model at extreme speed — the strongest choice.

models.name: Llama 4 Scout 17B
models.note: Light, fast, capable for simpler sites.

models.name: Llama 3.3 70B
models.note: Reliable open model, very cheap.

costNote: Free tier covers first sites at $0 within daily limits; paid open-model rates make a complete website cost well under ten cents.

steps.name: Create a Cerebras API key
steps.text: Sign up at cloud.cerebras.ai and create a key — the free tier requires no payment details.

steps.name: Optionally upgrade for higher limits
steps.text: Paid tiers unlock higher daily token budgets; set alerts in the dashboard.

steps.name: Connect the key to OpenThorn
steps.text: Sign up free at openthorn.app, open the Providers page, choose Cerebras, and paste your key. It is encrypted server-side with AES-256-GCM and never exposed raw.

steps.name: Describe your website and ship it
steps.text: Create a project, pick a model, and watch generation stream at thousands of tokens per second. Preview, iterate, then deploy or export.

faqs.question: Why is Cerebras so fast?
faqs.answer: Cerebras builds wafer-scale chips — entire silicon wafers acting as one processor — which serve open models at thousands of tokens per second, typically the fastest inference commercially available. For an iterative agent like OpenThorn's, that means near-instant build cycles.

faqs.question: Can I use Cerebras with OpenThorn for free?
faqs.answer: Yes — Cerebras offers a free API tier with daily token limits and no credit card, and OpenThorn is free, so your first websites cost $0.

faqs.question: Is my Cerebras API key safe in OpenThorn?
faqs.answer: Keys are encrypted server-side with AES-256-GCM using per-user derived keys and are never exposed raw to the browser. You can rotate the key in the Cerebras dashboard at any time.

name: Azure OpenAI
title: Build a website with Azure OpenAI
description: Connect your Azure OpenAI deployment to OpenThorn and build complete websites with GPT models under your organization's Azure governance, regions, and billing.
answer: Yes — OpenThorn supports Azure OpenAI, so teams can build websites with GPT models under existing Azure governance: your subscription, your region, your compliance boundary, your billing. Deploy a model in Azure AI Foundry, paste the endpoint key and deployment name into OpenThorn, and build.
freeTier: No free tier, but usage bills to your existing Azure subscription — new Azure accounts often include trial credit.

models.name: Your GPT deployment
models.note: Azure uses named deployments — deploy any available GPT model and use its deployment name in OpenThorn.

costNote: Per-token rates mirror OpenAI's, billed through your Azure subscription — a typical site costs cents to a few dollars depending on the deployed model.

steps.name: Create an Azure OpenAI resource
steps.text: In Azure AI Foundry (ai.azure.com), create an Azure OpenAI resource in your preferred region — this is where data residency is decided.

steps.name: Deploy a model
steps.text: Create a deployment of a GPT model and note its deployment name — Azure routes API calls by deployment, not model ID.

steps.name: Connect to OpenThorn
steps.text: Sign up free at openthorn.app, open the Providers page, choose Azure OpenAI, and enter your endpoint, key, and deployment name. Everything is encrypted server-side.

steps.name: Describe your website and ship it
steps.text: Create a project and describe the site. Usage bills to your Azure subscription under your existing governance. Deploy to Netlify or export the source.

faqs.question: Why use Azure OpenAI instead of OpenAI directly?
faqs.answer: Governance. Azure OpenAI runs GPT models inside your Azure subscription with your choice of region, your compliance certifications, your private networking, and consolidated enterprise billing — the standard route for organizations with Azure agreements.

faqs.question: What is a deployment name and why does OpenThorn need it?
faqs.answer: Azure routes API calls to named model deployments rather than raw model IDs. You create a deployment in Azure AI Foundry and give OpenThorn its name along with the endpoint and key.

faqs.question: Does Azure OpenAI cost more than OpenAI?
faqs.answer: Per-token rates are generally equivalent to OpenAI's; you pay through your Azure subscription. The value is governance and data residency rather than price.

name: Amazon Bedrock
title: Build a website with Amazon Bedrock
description: Connect Amazon Bedrock to OpenThorn and build complete websites with Claude models running inside your AWS account — your IAM controls, your region, your billing.
answer: Yes — OpenThorn supports Amazon Bedrock, so you can build websites with Claude models running inside your own AWS account: IAM-scoped credentials, your chosen region, CloudWatch auditability, and consolidated AWS billing. Enable model access in the Bedrock console, create scoped IAM credentials, and connect them to OpenThorn.
freeTier: No free tier; usage bills to your AWS account at per-token rates similar to Anthropic's direct prices.

models.name: Claude Opus 4.8
models.note: Anthropic's strongest Bedrock model for the most demanding builds.

models.name: Claude Sonnet 4.6
models.note: The best default — excellent code quality at mid-tier pricing.

costNote: Per-token rates are close to Anthropic's direct prices, billed through AWS — a typical site costs $0.50–$2 with Claude Sonnet 4.6.

steps.name: Enable model access in Bedrock
steps.text: In the AWS Bedrock console, request access to the Claude models in your preferred region — approval is typically immediate.

steps.name: Create scoped IAM credentials
steps.text: Create an IAM user or role with bedrock:InvokeModel permission only, and generate an access key pair. Least-privilege keys limit blast radius.

steps.name: Connect to OpenThorn
steps.text: Sign up free at openthorn.app, open the Providers page, choose Amazon Bedrock, and enter your credentials and region. Everything is encrypted server-side with AES-256-GCM.

steps.name: Describe your website and ship it
steps.text: Create a project, pick a Claude model, and describe the site. Usage appears in your AWS bill and CloudWatch logs. Deploy to Netlify or export the source.

faqs.question: Why use Bedrock instead of the Anthropic API directly?
faqs.answer: AWS governance: IAM-scoped access, your chosen region for data residency, CloudWatch audit trails, and one consolidated AWS bill. Per-token prices are close to Anthropic's direct rates, so you pay for control, not extra margin.

faqs.question: Which Bedrock model should I use for websites?
faqs.answer: Claude Sonnet 4.6 is the best default — one of the strongest coding models available at mid-tier pricing. Use Claude Opus 4.8 for the most complex multi-page builds.

faqs.question: What IAM permissions does OpenThorn need?
faqs.answer: Only bedrock:InvokeModel on the models you intend to use. Create a dedicated least-privilege IAM user so the credentials can do nothing else in your account.

name: NVIDIA NIM
title: Build a website with your NVIDIA NIM API key
description: Connect your NVIDIA NIM API key to OpenThorn and build websites with GPU-optimized open models — NVIDIA's catalog includes free developer credits to start.
answer: Yes — NVIDIA's NIM API serves GPU-optimized open models (DeepSeek R1, Nemotron) through build.nvidia.com, and new developer accounts include free credits. Create a key, paste it into OpenThorn (free, encrypted server-side), describe your site, and build your first pages at no cost.
freeTier: Yes — developer accounts receive free credits on build.nvidia.com to evaluate models.

models.name: DeepSeek R1
models.note: Strong reasoning model served on NVIDIA infrastructure.

models.name: Llama 3.1 Nemotron Ultra 253B
models.note: NVIDIA's tuned large model — capable for complex builds.

costNote: Free developer credits cover first sites at $0; afterwards, open-model rates keep a typical website in the cents range.

steps.name: Create an NVIDIA API key
steps.text: Sign up at build.nvidia.com — developer accounts include free credits — and generate an API key from the model catalog.

steps.name: Pick a model from the catalog
steps.text: Browse build.nvidia.com for available models; DeepSeek R1 and Nemotron are strong choices for code.

steps.name: Connect the key to OpenThorn
steps.text: Sign up free at openthorn.app, open the Providers page, choose NVIDIA NIM, and paste your key. It is encrypted server-side with AES-256-GCM and never exposed raw.

steps.name: Describe your website and ship it
steps.text: Create a project, pick a model, and describe the site. Preview it live, iterate, then deploy to Netlify or export the source.

faqs.question: Can I use NVIDIA NIM with OpenThorn for free?
faqs.answer: Yes — new developer accounts on build.nvidia.com include free credits, and OpenThorn is free, so your first website builds cost $0.

faqs.question: Which NVIDIA-served model is best for website generation?
faqs.answer: DeepSeek R1 is the strongest for reasoning-heavy builds; Llama 3.1 Nemotron Ultra is NVIDIA's tuned large model and handles complete sites well.

faqs.question: Is my NVIDIA API key safe in OpenThorn?
faqs.answer: Keys are encrypted server-side with AES-256-GCM using per-user derived keys and are never exposed raw to the browser. You can rotate the key on build.nvidia.com at any time.
